---
title: "Data Dictionary for Facility Reporting Variability Indicators"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: lualatex
    mainfont: "Work Sans"
    fontsize: 12pt
    geometry: margin=1in
    number-sections: false
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    toc: false
---

# `facility`
Name of the Iowa trauma facility. Use as a grouping key for all reporting and anomaly detection analyses.

# `2024`
Total number of trauma registry records submitted by the facility for calendar year 2024.

# `2025`
Total number of trauma registry records submitted by the facility for calendar year 2025.

# `diff_2025`
Change in reporting from 2024 to 2025. Computed as 2025 − 2024. Positive values indicate increased reporting; negative values indicate decreases.

# `mean_records`
Arithmetic mean of annual reporting volumes across 2020–2025. Used to contextualize reporting levels relative to historical patterns.

# `mean_diff`
Mean of annual year‑to‑year differences across 2021–2025. Indicates typical annual variability for the facility.

# `total_2020_2025`
Total trauma cases reported across the full 2020–2025 period. Proxy indicator of facility size and case load.

# `pct_2025`
Proportional change from 2024 to 2025. Computed as $\frac{(2025 − 2024)}{2024}$. Values above 1 indicate a >100% increase, and values below −1 indicate reporting decreased by more than 100%.

# `z_score_diff_2025`
Standardized 2025 difference score:
$\frac{(diff_{2025} − mean_{diff})}{standard\_deviation_{diff}}$
Used to identify unusually large deviations from each facility’s typical reporting variability.

# `z_anomaly_2025`
Logical flag indicating whether the 2025 difference is an extreme outlier based on Z‑score threshold (|Z| ≥ 1.5). Indicates statistically unusual reporting changes.

# `pct_anomaly_2025`
Logical flag indicating whether the proportional change exceeded ±50% relative to 2024. Useful for identifying large abrupt operational or data‑quality changes.
